# BitterClip

> BitterClip is a speaker-aware media workspace for turning long recordings
> into source-linked clips an AI agent can find and a human can verify before
> export or publishing.

BitterClip is part of the Bitter constellation of agent-operable software
systems. It applies Bitter's model of human-verifiable agent work to recorded
media: transcripts, speaker identity, source-linked Moments, rendered clips,
and publishing workflows.

## Canonical Pages

- [BitterClip home](https://bitterclip.com/): Product overview, positioning,
  theme-aware live editor demo, pricing, and signup link.
- [How BitterClip works](https://bitterclip.com/docs): Recording -> Moment ->
  Clip explanation, source timing model, media custody, and developer pointer.
- [MCP and AI assistant workflow](https://bitterclip.com/mcp): How ChatGPT,
  Claude, and MCP hosts can open the transcript editor and call BitterClip
  tools.
- [Privacy policy](https://bitterclip.com/privacy): How BitterClip handles
  account data, media, transcripts, clips, connector access, analytics, and
  publishing integrations.
- [Terms of service](https://bitterclip.com/terms): Terms for using
  BitterClip, including accounts, workspace media, publishing integrations,
  billing, acceptable use, and service availability.

## Markdown Alternates

Markdown alternates are clean text versions of the public pages. They are meant
for agents, crawlers, and public repository indexing.

- [Home Markdown](https://bitterclip.com/index.md): Markdown version of the
  BitterClip home page.
- [How it works Markdown](https://bitterclip.com/docs.md): Markdown version of
  the product model.
- [MCP Markdown](https://bitterclip.com/mcp.md): Markdown version of the MCP and
  AI assistant workflow page.
- [Privacy Markdown](https://bitterclip.com/privacy.md): Markdown version of the
  privacy policy.
- [Terms Markdown](https://bitterclip.com/terms.md): Markdown version of the
  terms of service.
- [Full Markdown bundle](https://bitterclip.com/llms-full.txt): Combined
  machine-readable BitterClip context in one file.

## Repository Context

- [Public repository](https://github.com/sheetgenius/bitterclip-marketing):
  Source for the public BitterClip website, Markdown mirrors, sitemap, README,
  changelog, and deployment wrapper.
- [Changelog](https://github.com/sheetgenius/bitterclip-marketing/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md):
  Semantic public change history for the website and repository context.
- [Contributing](https://github.com/sheetgenius/bitterclip-marketing/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md):
  Change checklist for public page, metadata, deployment, and discovery updates.
- [Security and public boundary](https://github.com/sheetgenius/bitterclip-marketing/blob/main/SECURITY.md):
  Sensitive-reporting and public/private boundary guidance.
- [Bitter](https://bitter.sh/): The broader constellation of agent-operable
  software systems.
- [BitterGrid](https://bittergrid.com/): Deployment substrate used to publish
  BitterClip.com from the public repository.

## Product Boundary

BitterClip is not a generic AI shorts generator. It is a speaker-aware media
workspace for recorded conversations. The public marketing repository does not
contain the private product application, media-processing backend, customer data,
provider payloads, billing system, or private operational runbooks.
